fr.elements: Main Building; Roof System; Front Driveway; Landscaped Area; Perimeter Fencing
fr.considerations: Dense Vegetation; Limited Rear Access; Single Access Point
fr.recommendations: Improving the clarity of entry and exit points, especially given the single access point, could enhance the arrival and departure of emergency personnel. Strategies such as widening or clearly marking the front driveway may assist in providing unhindered access. Trimming or managing the dense vegetation can improve visibility around the property which would enhance the ability of responders to assess situations quickly upon arrival. Addressing the limited rear access by considering additional access points can offer alternative routes for movement, supporting broader navigational options for emergency vehicles. If feasible, maintaining clear perimeter areas, particularly around any existing fencing and landscaped zones, could facilitate better staging and maneuverability on the property during emergency operations.

wildfire.conditions: Scattered Trees; Vegetation Adjacent to Structure; Vegetated Perimeter; Tree Cluster; Open Terrain; Defensible Space; Vegetation Adjacent to Access Route; Scattered Shrubs; Vegetation Adjacent to Structure; Vegetation Between Structures
wildfire.recommendations: Enhancing defensible space by managing scattered trees and shrubs near the structure could reduce fuel continuity and improve property preparedness. Regularly trimming and maintaining vegetation adjacent to the structure and access routes may reduce structure exposure and support firefighter access, facilitating suppression operations. Consider increasing separation between vegetated areas and the structure, as well as between clustered trees, to minimize opportunities for fire spread. Preserving open terrain and managing vegetation adjacent to access routes could further support effective access routes for firefighters and ensure long-term property resilience. Additionally, addressing the vegetation between structures by reducing its density can contribute to improved defensible space and create conditions more favorable for preventing fire transmission. Focus on maintaining clear zones and open spaces around property improvements to enhance wildfire mitigation efforts.
